key_insight: The paper proposes an intelligent closed-loop approach to reduce laser welding deviation in skin-frame structures by combining soft-voting ensemble learning with PSO-optimized fuzzy control.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for improving weld path accuracy and consistency in thin-walled aerospace or automotive assemblies where geometric deviation can degrade joint quality.
method: Use soft-voting ensemble learning to estimate or classify welding deviation states.
Optimize a fuzzy controller with particle swarm optimization (PSO) for adaptive deviation correction.
Integrate the predictor and controller into an intelligent regulation workflow for laser welding.
evidence_signal: The abstract identifies this as a journal article in Optics & Laser Technology, but it does not provide quantitative results or dataset details in the summary.
watch_item: Performance and robustness may depend on the representativeness of training data and on how well the controller generalizes to new skin-frame geometries and process disturbances.
paper_title: Intelligent regulation of laser welding deviation for skin-frame structures based on soft voting ensemble learning and PSO-optimized fuzzy control
